description: Comprehensive application of a bandage procedure and assessment
objectives: Demonstrate make sure the person is comfortable and tell them ...
Execute work from the side of the injury so you don't have...

steps.title: Make sure the person is comfortable and tell them ...
steps.description: Make sure the person is comfortable and tell them what you're doing.
key_points: Make sure the person is comfortable and tell them what you're doing.

steps.title: Work from the side of the injury so you don't have...
steps.description: Work from the side of the injury so you don't have to lean across their body.
key_points: Work from the side of the injury so you don't have to lean across their body.

steps.title: Keep the injured part of the body supported in the...
steps.description: Keep the injured part of the body supported in the position it'll be in when the bandage is on.
key_points: Keep the injured part of the body supported in the position it'll be in when the bandage is on.

steps.title: Use the right size bandage – different parts of th...
steps.description: Use the right size bandage – different parts of the body need different widths of bandage.
key_points: Use the right size bandage – different parts of the body need different widths of bandage.

steps.title: Avoid covering fingers or toes when bandaging a li...
steps.description: Avoid covering fingers or toes when bandaging a limb so you can easily check the circulation.
key_points: Avoid covering fingers or toes when bandaging a limb so you can easily check the circulation.

steps.title: Apply the bandage firmly, but not tightly, and sec...
steps.description: Apply the bandage firmly, but not tightly, and secure the end by folding it over and tying a knot in the end. You can also use a safety pin, tape or a bandage clip.
key_points: Apply the bandage firmly, but not tightly, and secure the end by folding it over and tying a knot in the end. You can also use a safety pin, tape or a bandage clip.

steps.title: As soon as the bandage is on, ask if it feels too ...
steps.description: As soon as the bandage is on, ask if it feels too tight and check the circulation by pressing on a fingernail or a piece of skin until it turns pale. If the colour doesn't return straight away, the bandage may be too tight, so you should loosen it. Limbs can swell up after an injury, so check the circulation every 10 minutes after you have put the bandage on.

name: Prediction of difficult direct endotracheal intubation
description: Comprehensive prediction of difficult direct endotracheal intubation procedure and assessment
objectives: Demonstrate m – mallampati classification: in the awake, sitti...
Execute m – measures 3-3-1: student assess the thyromental...

steps.title: M – Mallampati classification: in the awake, sitti...
steps.description: M – Mallampati classification: in the awake, sitting and cooperative patient, the student asks the patient to open his mouth fully, and to extrude the tongue without phonating. The student then verbalises what structures are visible and scores the patient according to the class. The student then reports on the predicted difficulty.
key_points: M – Mallampati classification: in the awake, sitting and cooperative patient, the student asks the patient to open his mouth fully, and to extrude the tongue without phonating. The student then verbalises what structures are visible and scores the patient according to the class. The student then reports on the predicted difficulty.

steps.title: M – Measures 3-3-1: student assess the thyromental...
steps.description: M – Measures 3-3-1: student assess the thyromental span by placing three fingers breadths (patient’s diameter) between the patient’s mentum of the chin and superior border of the thyroid cartilage. Student then opens the patient’s mouth and should be able to place 3 finger breadths between the teeth of the mandible and the teeth of the maxilla.
key_points: M – Measures 3-3-1: student assess the thyromental span by placing three fingers breadths (patient’s diameter) between the patient’s mentum of the chin and superior border of the thyroid cartilage. Student then opens the patient’s mouth and should be able to place 3 finger breadths between the teeth of the mandible and the teeth of the maxilla.

steps.title: A – Atlanto –occipital extension: in the absence o...
steps.description: A – Atlanto –occipital extension: in the absence of C-spine precautions the student flexes the patient’s neck at the cervico-thoracic junction, extends the head at the atlanto-occipital junction and reports on the range of motion.
key_points: A – Atlanto –occipital extension: in the absence of C-spine precautions the student flexes the patient’s neck at the cervico-thoracic junction, extends the head at the atlanto-occipital junction and reports on the range of motion.

steps.title: P - Pathology: student examines patient and report...
steps.description: P - Pathology: student examines patient and reports on any pathology that may be obstructing the airway (medical or traumatic origin)
key_points: P - Pathology: student examines patient and reports on any pathology that may be obstructing the airway (medical or traumatic origin)
